author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: 1 John III. 19–IV. 3.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And herein we know that we are of the truth, and assure our hearts before Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if our heart think ill of us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Beloved, if our heart think not ill of us, then have we confidence toward God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And whatsoever we ask, we shall receive of Him, because we keep His commandments, and do in His sight those things that please Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And this is His commandment, That we should believe on the name of His Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, as He gave us commandment.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And he that keepeth His commandments shall dwell in Him, and He in him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And herein we know that He abideth in us, by the Holy Spirit which He hath given us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Dearly beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they are of God: because many false prophets are gone out into this world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In this is known the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God: and every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God: and this is the antichrist, of whom ye have heard that he should come; and even now already is he in this world.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If ye remember, brethren, yesterday we closed our sermon at this sentence, which without doubt behooved and does behoove to abide in your heart, seeing it was the last ye heard.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “My little children, let us not love only in word and in tongue; but in deed and in truth.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then he goes on: “And herein we know that we are of the truth, and assure our hearts before Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “For if our heart think ill of us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He had said, “Let us not love only in word and in tongue, but in work and in truth:” we are asked, In what work, or in what truth, is he known that loveth God, or loveth his brother?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Above he had said up to what point charity is perfected: what the Lord saith in the Gospel, “Greater love than this hath no man, that one lay down his life for his friends,” this same had the apostle also said: “As He laid down His life for us, we ought also to lay down our lives for the brethren.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This is the perfection of charity, and greater can not at all be found.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But because it is not perfect in all, and that man ought not to despair in whom it is not perfect, if that be already born which may be perfected: and of course if born, it must be nourished, and by certain nourishments of its own must be brought unto its proper perfection: therefore, we have asked concerning the commencement of charity, where it begins, and there have straightway found: “But whoso hath this world’s goods, and seeth his brother have need, and shutteth up his bowels of compassion from him, how dwelleth the love of the Father in him?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Here then hath this charity, my brethren, its beginning: to give of one’s superfluities to him that hath need to him that is in any distress; of one’s temporal abundance to deliver his brother from temporal tribulation.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Here is the first rise of charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This, being thus begun, if thou shalt nourish with the word of God and hope of the life to come, thou wilt come at last unto that perfection, that thou shalt be ready to lay down thy life for thy brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But, because many such things are done by men who seek other objects, and who love not the brethren; let us come back to the tes timony of conscience.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How do we prove that many such things are done by men who love not the brethren?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How many in heresies and schisms call themselves martyrs!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: They seem to themselves to lay down their lives for their brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If for the brethren they laid down their lives, they would not separate themselves from the whole brotherhood.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Again, how many there are who for the sake of vainglory bestow much, give much, and seek therein but the praise of men and popular glory, which is full of windiness, and possesses no stability!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Seeing, then, there are such, where shall be the proof of brotherly charity?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Seeing he wished it to be proved, and hath said by way of admonition, “My little children, let us not love only in word and in tongue; but in deed and in truth;” we ask, in what work, in what truth?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Can there be a more manifest work than to give to the poor?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Many do this of vainglory, not of love.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Can there be a greater work than to die for the brethren?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This also, many would fain be thought to do, who do it of vainglory to get a name, not from bowels of love.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It remains, that that man loves his brother, who before God, where God alone seeth, assures his own heart, and questions his heart whether he does this indeed for love of the brethren; and his witness is that eye which penetrates the heart, where man cannot look.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore Paul the Apostle, because he was ready to die for the brethren, and said, “I will myself be spent for your souls,” yet, because God only saw this in his heart, not the mortal men to whom he spake, he saith to them, “But to me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you or at man’s bar.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And the same apostle shows also in a certain place, that these things are oft done of empty vainglory, not upon the solid ground of love: for speaking of the praises of charity he saith, “If I distribute all my goods to the poor, and if I deliver up my body to be burned, but have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it possible for a man to do this without charity?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For they that have divided unity, are persons that have not charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Seek there, and ye shall see many giving much to the poor; shall see others prepared to welcome death, insomuch that where there is no persecutor they cast themselves headlong: these doubtless without charity do this.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let us come back then to conscience, of which the apostle saith: “For our glorying is this, the testimony of our conscience.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let us come back to conscience, of which the same saith, “But let each prove his own work, and then he shall have glorying in himself and not in another.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore, let each one of us “prove his own work,” whether it flow forth from the vein of charity, whether it be from charity as the root that his good works sprout forth as branches.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “But let each prove his own work, and then he shall have glorying in himself and not in another,” not when another’s tongue bears witness to him, but when his own conscience bears it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This it is then that he enforces here.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “In this we know that we are of the truth, when in deed and in truth” we love, “not only in words and in tongue: and assure our heart before Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What meaneth, “before Him?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Where He seeth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence the Lord Himself in the Gospel saith: “Take heed that ye do not your righteousness before men, to be seen of them: otherwise ye have no reward with your Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what meaneth, “Let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:” except that the right hand means a pure conscience, the left hand the lust of the world?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Many through lust of the world do many wonderful things: the left hand worketh, not the right.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The right hand ought to work, and without knowledge of the left hand, so that lust of the world may not even mix itself therewith when by love we work aught that is good.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And where do we get to know this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou art before God: question thine heart, see what thou hast done, and what therein was thine aim; thy salvation, or the windy praise of men.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Look within, for man cannot judge whom he cannot see.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If “we assure our heart,” let it be “before Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because “if our heart think ill of us,” i.e. accuse us within, that we do not the thing with that mind it ought to be done withal, “greater is God than our heart, and knoweth all things.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou hidest thine heart from man: hide it from God if thou canst!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How shalt thou hide it from Him, to whom it is said by a sinner, fearing and confessing, “Whither shall I go from Thy Spirit? and from Thy face whither shall I flee?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He sought a way to flee, to escape the judgment of God, and found none.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For where is God not?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “If I shall ascend,” saith he, “into heaven, Thou art there: if I shall descend into hell, Thou art there.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whither wilt thou go? whither wilt thou flee?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wilt thou hear counsel?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thou wouldest flee from Him, flee to Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Flee to Him by confessing, not from Him by hiding: hide thou canst not, but confess thou canst.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Say unto Him, “Thou art my place to flee unto;” and let love be nourished in thee, which alone leadeth unto life.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let thy conscience bear thee witness that thy love is of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If it be of God, do not wish to display it before men; because neither men’s praises lift thee unto heaven, nor their censures put thee down from thence.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let Him see, who crowneth thee: be He thy witness, by whom as judge thou art crowned.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Greater is God than our heart, and knoweth all things.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Beloved, if our heart think not ill of us, we have confidence towards God:”—What meaneth, “If our heart think not ill”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If it make true answer to us, that we love and that there is genuine love in us: not feigned but sincere; seeking a brother’s salvation, expecting no emolument from a brother, but only his salvation—“we have confidence toward God: and whatsoever we ask, we shall receive of Him, because we keep His commandments.”—Therefore, not in the sight of men, but where God Himself seeth, in the heart—“we have confidence,” then, “towards God: and whatsoever we ask, we shall receive of Him:” howbeit, because we keep His commandments.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What are “His commandments”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Must we be always repeating?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “A new commandment give I unto you, that ye love one another.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is charity itself that he speaks of, it is this that he enforces.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whoso then shall have brotherly charity, and have it before God, where God seeth, and his heart being interrogated under righteous examination make him none other answer than that the genuine root of charity is there for good fruits to come from; that man hath confidence with God, and whatsoever he shall ask, he shall receive of Him, because he keepeth His commandments.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Here a question meets us: for it is not this or that man, or thou or I that come in question,—for if I have asked any thing of God and receive it not, any person may easily say of me, “He hath not charity:” and of any man soever of this present time, this may easily be said; and let any think what he will, a man of man:—not we, but those come more in question, those men of whom it is on all hands known that they were saints when they wrote, and that they are now with God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Where is the man that hath charity, if Paul had it not, who said, “Our mouth is open unto you, O ye Corinthians, our heart is enlarged; ye are not straitened in us:” who said, “I will myself be spent for your souls:” and so great grace was in him, that it was manifested that he had charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And yet we find that he asked and did not receive.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What say we, brethren?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is a question: look attentively to God: it is a great question, this also.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Just as, where it was said of sin, “He that is born of God sinneth not:” we found this sin to be the violating of charity, and that this was the thing strictly intended in that place: so too we ask now what it is that he would say.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if thou look but to the words, it seems plain: if thou take the examples into the account, it is obscure.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Than the words here nothing can be plainer.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And whatsoever we ask, we shall receive of Him, because we keep His commandments, and do those things that are pleasing in His sight.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Whatsoever we ask,” saith he, “we shall receive of Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He hath put us sorely to straits.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the other place also he would put us to straits, if he meant all sin: but then we found room to expound it in this, that he meant it of a certain sin, not of all sin; howbeit of a sin which “whosoever is born of God committeth not:” and we found that this same sin is none other than the violation of charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We have also a manifest example from the Gospel, when the Lord saith, “If I had not come, they had not had sin.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Were the Jews innocent when He came to them, because He so speaks?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then if He had not come, would they have had no sin?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then did the Physician’s presence make one sick, not take away the fever?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What madman even would say this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He came not but to cure and heal the sick.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore when He said, “If I had not come, they had not had sin,” what would He have to be understood, but a certain sin in particular?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For there was a sin which the Jews would not have had.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That they believed not on Him, that when he had come they despised Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: As then He there said “sin,” and it does not follow that we are to understand all sin, but a certain sin: so here also not all sin, lest it be contrary to that place where he saith, “If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us:” but a certain sin in particular, that is, the violation of charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But in this place he hath bound us more tightly: “If we shall ask,” he hath said, “if our heart accuse us not, and tell us in answer, in the sight of God, that true love is in us;” “Whatsoever we ask, we shall receive of Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Well now: I have already told you, my, beloved brethren, let no man turn toward us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For what are we? or what are ye?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What, but the Church of God which is known to all?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And, if it please Him, in that Church are we; and those of us who by love abide in it, there let us persevere, if we would show the love we have.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But then the apostle Paul, what evil are we to think of him?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He not love the brethren!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He not have within himself the testimony of his conscience in the sight of God!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Paul not have within him that root of charity whence all good fruits proceeded!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What madman would say this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Well then: where find we that the apostle asked and did not receive?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He saith himself: “Lest I should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, an angel of Satan to buffet me.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For which thing I besought the Lord thrice, that He would take it from me.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And He said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for strength is made perfect in weakness.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Lo, he was not heard in his prayer that the “angel of Satan” should be taken from him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But wherefore?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because it was not good for him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He was heard, then, for salvation, when he was not heard according to his wish.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Know, my beloved, a great mystery: which we urge upon your consideration on purpose that it may not slip from you in your temptations.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The saints are in all things heard unto salvation: they are always heard in that which respects their eternal salvation; it is this that they desire: because in regard of this, their prayers are always heard.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But let us distinguish God’s different ways of hearing prayer.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For we find some not heard for their wish, heard for salvation: and again some we find heard for their wish, not heard for salvation.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark this difference, hold fast this example of a man not heard for his wish but heard for salvation.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear the apostle Paul; for what is the hearing of prayer unto salvation, God Himself showed him: “Sufficient for thee,” saith He, “is my grace; for strength is perfected in weakness.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou hast besought, hast cried, hast thrice cried: the very cry thou didst raise once for all I heard, I turned not away mine ears from thee; I know what I should do: thou wouldest have it taken away, the healing thing by which thou art burned; I know the infirmity by which thou art burdened.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Well then: here is a man who was heard for salvation, while as to his will he was not heard.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Where find we persons heard for their will, not heard for salvation?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Do we find, think we, some wicked, some impious man, heard of God for his will, not heard for salvation?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If I put to you the instance of some man, perchance thou wilt say to me, “It is thou that callest him wicked, for he was righteous; had he not been righteous, his prayer would not have been heard by God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The instance I am about to allege is of one, of whose iniquity and impiety none can doubt.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The devil himself: he asked for Job, and received.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Have ye not here also heard concerning the devil, that “he that committeth sin is of the devil”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Not that the devil created, but that the sinner imitates.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it not said of him, “He stood not in the truth”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is not even he “that old serpent,” who, through the woman pledged the first man in the drink of poison?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Who even in the case of Job, kept for him his wife, that by her the husband might be, not comforted, but tempted?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The devil asked for a holy man, to tempt him; and he received: the apostle asked that the thorn in the flesh might be taken from him, and he received not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But the apostle was more heard than the devil.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the apostle was heard for salvation, though not for his wish: the devil was heard for his wish, but for damnation.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For that Job was yielded up to him to be tempted, was in order that by his standing the proof the devil should be tormented.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But this, my brethren, we find not only in the Old Testament books, but also in the Gospel.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The demons besought the Lord, when He expelled them from the man, that they might be permitted to go into the swine.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Should the Lord not have power to tell them not to approach even those creatures?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For, had it not been His will to permit this, they were not about to rebel against the King of heaven and earth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But with a view to a certain mystery, with a certain ulterior meaning, He let the demons go into the swine: to show that the devil hath dominion in them that lead the life of swine.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Demons then were heard in their request; was the apostle not heard?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Or rather (what is truer) shall we say, The apostle was heard, the demons not heard?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Their will was effected; his weal was perfected.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Agreeably with this, we ought to understand that God, though He give not to our will, doth give for our salvation.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For sup pose the thing thou have asked be to thine hurt, and the Physician knows that it is to thine hurt; what then?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is not to be said that the physician does not give ear to thee, when, perhaps, thou askest for cold water, and if it is good for thee, he gives it immediately, if not good, he gives it not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Had he no ears for thy request, or rather, did he give ear for thy weal, even when he gainsaid thy will?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then let there be in you charity, my brethren; let it be in you, and then set your minds at rest: even when the thing ye ask for is not given you, your prayer is granted, only, ye know it not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Many have been given into their own hands, to their own hurt: of whom the apostle saith, “God gave them up to their own hearts’ lusts.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Some man hath asked for a great sum of money; he hath received, to his hurt.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When he had it not, he had little to fear; no sooner did he come to have it, than he became a prey to the more powerful.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Was not that man’s request granted to his own hurt, who would needs have that for which he should be sought after by the robber, whereas, being poor, none sought after him?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Learn to beseech God that ye may commit it to the Physician to do what He knows best.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Do thou confess the disease, let Him apply the means of healing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Do thou only hold fast charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For He will needs cut, will needs burn; what if thou criest out, and art not spared for thy crying under the cutting, under the burning and the tribulation, yet He knows how far the rottenness reaches.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou wouldest have Him even now take off His hands, and He considers only the deepness of the sore; He knows how far to go.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He does not attend to thee for thy will, but he does attend to thee for thy healing.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Be ye sure, then, my brethren, that what the apostle saith is true: “For we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered: for He maketh intercession for the saints.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How is it said, “The Spirit itself intercedeth for the saints,” but as meaning the charity which is wrought in thee by the Spirit?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For therefore saith the same apostle: “The charity of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Spirit which is given unto us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is charity that groans, it is charity that prays: against it He who gave it cannot shut His ears.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Set your minds at rest: let charity ask, and the ears of God are there.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Not that which thou wishest is done, but that is done which is advantageous.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore, “whatever we ask,” saith he, “we shall receive of Him,” I have already said, If thou understand it to mean, “for salvation,” there is no question: if not for salvation, there is a question, and a great one, a question that makes thee an accuser of the apostle Paul.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Whatever we ask, we receive of Him, because we keep His commandments, and do these things that are pleasing in His sight:” within, where He seeth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what are those commandments?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “This,” saith he, “is His commandment, That we should believe on the name of His Son Jesus Christ, and love one another.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye see that this is the commandment: ye see that whoso doeth aught against this commandment, doeth the sin from which “every one that is born of God” is free.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “As He gave us commandment:” that we love one another.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And he that keepeth His commandment”—ye see that none other thing is bidden us than that we love one another—“And he that keepeth His commandment shall abide in Him, and He in him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And in this we know that He abideth in us, by the Spirit which He hath given us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it not manifest that this is what the Holy Ghost works in man, that there should be in him love and charity?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it not manifest, as the Apostle Paul saith, that “the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given us”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For [our apostle] was speaking of charity, and was saying that we ought in the sight of God to interrogate our own heart.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “But if our heart think not ill of us:” i.e. if it confess that from the love of our brother is done in us whatever is done in any good work.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And then besides, in speaking of the commandment, he says this: “This is His commandment, That we should believe on the name of His Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, as He gave us commandment.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And he that doeth His commandment abideth in Him, and He in him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In this we know that He abideth in us, by the Spirit which He hath given us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If in truth thou find that thou hast charity, thou hast the Spirit of God in order to understand: for a very necessary thing it is.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the earliest times, “the Holy Ghost fell upon them that believed: and they spake with tongues,” which they had not learned, “as the Spirit gave them utterance.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These were signs adapted to the time.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For there behooved to be that betokening of the Holy Spirit in all tongues, to shew that the Gospel of God was to run through all tongues over the whole earth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That thing was done for a betokening, and it passed away.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the laying on of hands now, that persons may receive the Holy Ghost, do we look that they should speak with tongues?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Or when we laid the hand on these infants, did each one of you look to see whether they would speak with tongues, and, when he saw that they did not speak with tongues, was any of you so wrong-minded as to say, These have not received the Holy Ghost; for, had they received, they would speak with tongues as was the case in those times?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If then the witness of the presence of the Holy Ghost be not now given through these miracles, by what is it given, by what does one get to know that he has received the Holy Ghost?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let him question his own heart.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If he love his brother the Spirit of God dwelleth in him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let him see, let him prove himself before the eyes of God, let him see whether there be in him the love of peace and unity, the love of the Church that is spread over the whole earth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let him not rest only in his loving the brother whom he has before his eyes, for we have many brethren whom we do not see, and in the unity of the Spirit we are joined to them.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What marvel that they are not with us?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We are in one body, we have one Head, in heaven.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Brethren, our two eyes do not see each other; as one may say, they do not know each other.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But in the charity of the bodily frame do they not know each other?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For, to shew you that in the charity which knits them together they do know each other; when both eyes are open, the right may not rest on some object, on which the left shall not rest likewise.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Direct the glance of the right eye without the other, if thou canst.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Together they meet in one object, together they are directed to one object: their aim is one, their places diverse.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If then all who with thee love God have one aim with thee, heed not that in the body thou are separated in place; the eyesight of the heart ye have alike fixed on the light of truth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then if thou wouldest know that thou hast received the Spirit, question thine heart: lest haply thou have the sacrament, and have not the virtue of the sacrament.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Question thine heart.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If love of thy brethren be there, set thy mind at rest.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There cannot be love without the Spirit of God: since Paul cries, “The love of God is shed abroad in your hearts by the Holy Spirit which is given unto us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Beloved, believe not every spirit.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because he had said, “In this we know that He abideth in us, by the Spirit which He hath given us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But how this same Spirit is known, mark this: “Beloved, believe not every spirit, but prove the spirits whether they be from God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And who is he that proves the spirits?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A hard matter has he put to us, my brethren!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is well for us that he should tell us himself how we are to discern them.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He is about to tell us: fear not: but first see; mark: see that hereby is expressed the very thing that vain heretics taunt us withal.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark, see what he says, “Beloved, believe not every spirit, but prove the spirits whether they be from God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The Holy Spirit is spoken of in the Gospel by the name of water; where the Lord “cried and said, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He that believeth on me, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But the evangelist has expounded of what He said this: for he goes on to say, “But this spake He of the Spirit, which they that believed on Him should receive.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wherefore did not the Lord baptize many?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But what saith he?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “For the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then seeing those had baptism, and had not yet received the Holy Ghost, whom on the day of Pentecost the Lord sent from heaven, the glorifying of the Lord was first waited for, so that the Spirit might be given.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Even before He was glorified, and before He sent the Spirit, He yet invited men to prepare themselves for the receiving of the water of which He said, “Whoso thirsteth, let him come and drink;” and, “He that believeth on me, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What meaneth, “Rivers of living water”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What is that water?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let no man ask me; ask the Gospel.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “But this,” saith it, “He said of the Spirit, which they should receive that should believe on Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Consequently, the water of the sacrament is one thing: another, the water which betokens the Spirit of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The water of the sacrament is visible: the water of the Spirit invisible.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That washes the body, and betokens that which is done in the soul.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By this Spirit the soul itself is cleansed and fed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This is the Spirit of God, which heretics and all that cut themselves off from the Church, cannot have.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And whosoever do not openly cut themselves off, but by iniquity are cut off, and being within, whirl about as chaff and are not grain; these have not this Spirit.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This Spirit is denoted by the Lord under the name of water: and we have heard from this epistle, “Believe not every spirit;” and those words of Solomon bear witness, “From strange water keep thee far.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What meaneth, “water”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Does water always signify spirit?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Not always: but in some places it signifies the Spirit, in some places it signifies baptism, in some places signifies peoples, in some places signifies counsel: thus thou findest it said in a certain place, “Counsel is a fountain of life to them that possess it.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So then, in divers places of the Scriptures, the term “water” signifies divers things.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now however by the term water ye have heard the Holy Spirit spoken of, not by an interpretation of ours but by witness of the Gospel, where it saith, “But this said He of the Spirit, which they should receive that should believe on Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If then by the name of water is signified the Holy Spirit, and this epistle saith to us, “Believe not every spirit, but prove the spirits, whether they be of God;” let us understand that of this it is said, “From strange water keep thee far, and from a strange fountain drink thou not.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What meaneth, “From a strange fountain drink thou not”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A strange spirit believe thou not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There remains then the test by which it is to be proved to be the Spirit of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He has indeed set down a sign, and this, belike, difficult: let us see, however.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We are to recur to that charity; it is that which teacheth us, because it is the unction.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: However, what saith he here?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Prove the spirits, whether they be from God: because many false prophets have gone out into this world.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now there are all heretics and all schismatics.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How then am I to prove the spirit?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He goes on: “In this is known the Spirit of God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wake up the ears of your heart.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We were at a loss; we were saying, Who knows? who discerns?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Behold, he is about to tell the sign.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Hereby is known the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God: and every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God: and this is the antichrist, of whom ye have heard that he should come; and even now already is he in this world.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Our ears, so to say, are on the alert for discerning of the spirits; and we have been told something, such that thereby we discern not a whit the more.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For what saith he?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ came in the flesh, is of God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then is the spirit that is among the heretics, of God, seeing they “confess that Jesus Christ came in the flesh”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Aye, here perchance they lift themselves up against us, and say: Ye have not the Spirit from God; but we confess “that Jesus Christ came in the flesh:” but the apostle here hath said that those have not the Spirit of God, who confess not “that Jesus Christ came in the flesh.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ask the Arians: they confess “that Jesus Christ came in the flesh:” ask the Eunomians; they confess “that Jesus Christ came in the flesh:” ask the Macedonians; they confess “that Jesus Christ came in the flesh:” put the question to the Cataphryges; they confess “that Jesus Christ came in the flesh:” put it to the Novatians; they confess “that Jesus Christ came in the flesh.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then have all these heresies the Spirit of God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Are they then no false prophets?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is there then no deception there, no seduction there?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Assuredly they are antichrists; for “they went out from us, but were not of us.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What are we to do then?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By what to discern them?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Be very attentive; let us go together in heart, and knock.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Charity herself keeps watch; for it is none other than she that shall knock, she also that shall open: anon ye shall understand in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Already ye have heard that it was said above, “Whoso denieth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh, the same is an antichrist.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There also we asked, Who denies? because neither do we deny, nor do those deny.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And we found that some do in their deeds deny; and we brought testimony from the apostle, who saith, “For they confess that they know God, but in their deeds deny Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thus then let us now also make the enquiry in the deeds not in the tongue.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What is the spirit that is not from God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That “which denieth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what is the spirit that is from God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That “which confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Who is he that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now, brethren, to the mark! let us look to the works, not stop at the noise of the tongue.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let us ask why Christ came in the flesh, so we get at the persons who deny that He is come in the flesh.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thou stop at tongues, why, thou shalt hear many a heresy confessing that Christ is come in the flesh: but the truth convicteth those men.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wherefore came Christ in the flesh?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Was He not God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it not written of Him, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Was it not He that did feed angels, is it not He that doth feed angels?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Did He not in such sort come hither, that He departed not thence?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Did He not in such sort ascend, that He forsook not us?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wherefore then came He in the flesh?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because it behooved us to have the hope of resurrection shown unto us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: God He was, and in flesh He came; for God could not die, flesh could die; He came then in the flesh, that He might die for us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But how died He for us?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Greater charity than this hath no man, that a man lay down his life for his friends.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Charity therefore brought Him to the flesh.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whoever therefore has not charity denies that Christ is come in the flesh.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Here then do thou now question all heretics.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Did Christ come in the flesh?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “He did come; this I believe, this I confess.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Nay, this thou deniest.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “How do I deny?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou hearest that I say it!”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Nay, I convict thee of denying it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou sayest with the voice, deniest with the heart; sayest in words, deniest in deeds.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “How,” sayest thou, “do I deny in deeds?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because the end for which Christ came in the flesh, was, that He might die for us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He died for us, because therein He taught much charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Greater charity than this hath no man, that a man lay down his life for his friends.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou hast not charity, seeing thou for thine own honor dividest unity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore by this understand ye the spirit that is from God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Give the earthen vessels a tap, put them to the proof, whether haply they be cracked and give a dull sound: see whether they ring full and clear, see whether charity be there.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou takest thyself away from the unity of the whole earth, thou dividest the Church by schisms, thou rendest the Body of Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He came in the flesh, to gather in one, thou makest an outcry to scatter abroad.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This then is the Spirit of God, which saith that Jesus is come in the flesh, which saith, not in tongue but in deeds, which saith, not by making a noise but by loving.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And that spirit is not of God, which denies that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh; denies, here also, not in tongue but in life; not in words but in deeds.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is manifest therefore by what we may know the brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Many within are in a sort within; but none without except he be indeed without.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Nay, and that ye may know that he has referred the matter to deeds, he saith, “And every spirit, qui solvit Christum, which does away with Christ that He came in the flesh, is not of God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A doing away in deeds is meant.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What has he shown thee?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “That denieth:” in that he saith, “doeth away” (or, “unmaketh”).

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He came to gather in one, thou comest to unmake.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou wouldest pull Christ’s members asunder.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How can it be said that thou deniest not that Christ is come in the flesh, who rendest assunder the Church of God which He hath gathered together?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore thou goest against Christ; thou art an antichrist.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Be thou within, or be thou without, thou art an antichrist: only, when thou art within, thou art hidden; when thou art without, thou art made manifest.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou unmakest Jesus and deniest that He came in the flesh; thou art not of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore He saith in the Gospel: “Whoso shall break one of these least commandments, and shall teach so, shall be called least in the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What is this breaking?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What this teaching?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A breaking in the deeds and a teaching as it were in words.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Thou that preachest men should not steal, dost thou steal?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore he that steals breaks or undoes the commandment in his deed, and as it were teaches so: “he shall be called least in the kingdom of heaven,” i.e. in the Church of this present time.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Of him it is said, “What they say do ye; but what they do, that do not ye.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But he that shall do, and shall teach so, shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: From this, that He has here said, fecerit, “shall do,” while in opposition to this He has there said solverit, meaning non fecerit, “shall not do, and shall teach so”—to break, then, is, not to do—what doth He teach us, but that we should interrogate men’s deeds, not take their words upon trust?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The obscurity of the things compels us to speak much at length, chiefly that that which the Lord deigns to reveal may be brought within reach even of the brethren of slower understanding, because all were bought by the blood of Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And I am afraid the epistle itself will not be finished during these days as I promised: but as the Lord will, it is better to reserve the remainder, than to overload your hearts with too much food.
